note: on each page load, draw 13 charms at random from the full pool. re-randomize on every visit.

charms.name: the witchy marble
charms.throughline: the marble doesn't care about arriving. it goes where it goes.

scores.title: the long way round
body: take the route that wastes the most time.
don't account for it when you arrive.

scores.title: no plan
body: choose one hour this week with nothing scheduled in it.
make no plan for it.
scores.closing: see what arrives.

scores.title: the incomplete
body: find something you abandoned.
spend five minutes with it without trying to finish it.
put it down again.
scores.closing: incompletion is also a form.

scores.title: the wrong answer
body: write down the wrong answer to a problem you're stuck on.
describe it fully.
scores.closing: see what it knows that the right answer doesn't.

charms.name: spool of thread
charms.throughline: thread connects two points. you can follow it back. the spool is wound up, waiting — more of it than you can see.

scores.title: the slow question
body: write a question you've been sitting with.
mail it to yourself.
answer it when it arrives.

scores.title: borrowed object
body: borrow something small from someone.
carry it for a day as though its history is partly yours.
return it before you explain what you were doing.

scores.title: postcard to the unknown
body: addressed to: whoever needs it.
write something neighborly.
leave it somewhere a stranger might find it.
scores.closing: the world reads back, eventually.

scores.title: carry it until
body: pick up an object near you.
carry it until someone asks about it.
tell them the true reason you chose it — even if you have to invent it in the moment of telling.

charms.name: stone
charms.throughline: older than everything else in the set. completely indifferent. it's about slowing all the way down until something arrives.

scores.title: half speed
body: do the next thing you were going to do at half the speed.
scores.closing: notice what you would have skipped.

scores.title: the slow walk
body: walk somewhere you normally hurry through.
match your pace to the slowest person you can see.

scores.title: ten minutes
body: set a timer for ten minutes.
do something with no output.
when it ends, resist the urge to account for it.
scores.closing: the ten minutes happened anyway.

scores.title: the drawer
body: write down the most obvious solution to a problem you have.
put it in a drawer.
don't look at it for three days.
scores.closing: see what arrived in the meantime.

charms.name: the frog clip
charms.throughline: it works even though it's silly. permission to be unserious about serious things.

scores.title: laugh incorrectly
body: laugh out loud at something only mildly funny.
stop when the laugh becomes real.
scores.closing: some emotions need a doorway.

scores.title: the wrong answer
body: write down the wrong answer to a problem you're stuck on.
describe it fully.
scores.closing: see what it knows that the right answer doesn't.

scores.title: the wrong hand
body: make something using only your non-dominant hand.
don't apologize for how it turns out.
scores.closing: the hand that doesn't know what it's doing often gets closer to the thing.

scores.title: no plan
body: choose one hour this week with nothing scheduled in it.
make no plan for it.
scores.closing: see what arrives.

charms.name: skeleton key
charms.throughline: the door you haven't tried yet. unlocking is only possible if you go to the door first.

scores.title: mark the threshold
body: mark a threshold you cross regularly.
it doesn't mean anything yet.
cross it every time you leave.
scores.closing: see if it starts to.

scores.title: the bad idea
body: write down the idea you've been dismissing as bad.
describe it fully, as though it were good.
scores.closing: see what it contains.

scores.title: before you're ready
body: identify something you're waiting until you're ready to do.
do a one-minute version of it now.
scores.closing: readiness is sometimes a story about the future that the body resolves in the present.

scores.title: not solo
body: the next thing you were going to do alone.
call a friend and stay on the phone while you do it.
make tea, take out the garbage, send an email.

charms.name: domino
charms.throughline: yet to be played. the space between knowing and being.

scores.title: i might be wrong
body: the next time you're certain you're right,
say i might be wrong about this before you continue.
scores.closing: notice if you continue differently.

scores.title: the gap
body: write down something you understand but haven't yet become.
fold the paper.
put it somewhere you'll find it in a week.
scores.closing: understanding and being-otherwise move at different speeds.

scores.title: the wrong expert
body: ask someone younger than you how they'd do something.
try their suggestion once.
scores.closing: expertise travels in one direction until it doesn't.

scores.title: the unfinished
body: find something you left half-done.
sit with it for five minutes without touching it.
leave it half-done again.

charms.name: button
charms.throughline: held something closed, past tense. now separated from its original function. things taken out of context.

scores.title: the rescued thing
body: find something you were about to discard.
keep it for one week.
scores.closing: see if your reason for discarding it changes.

scores.title: the gap
body: write down something you understand but haven't yet become.
fold the paper.
put it somewhere you'll find it in a week.
scores.closing: understanding and being-otherwise move at different speeds.

scores.title: the unfinished
body: find something you left half-done.
sit with it for five minutes without touching it.
leave it half-done again.

scores.title: laugh incorrectly
body: laugh out loud at something only mildly funny.
stop when the laugh becomes real.
scores.closing: some emotions need a doorway.

charms.name: shell
charms.throughline: carries the shape of what used to live in it. attending to what's already there before you arrived.

scores.title: the existing movement
body: look out a window.
find something that was already moving before you looked.
watch it until it does something you didn't expect.
scores.closing: the world rarely waits for our attention.

scores.title: the slow question
body: write a question you've been sitting with.
mail it to yourself.
answer it when it arrives.

scores.title: three things
body: find a living thing.
whisper three things you've almost stopped believing could still happen.
every time you tend to it, remember one.

scores.title: jar of small things
body: place a container somewhere visible.
each time something small but unexpected happens, note it.
open it at the end of the month and read it back.

charms.name: box of matches
charms.throughline: a timer you're holding. permission with a built-in expiration. you cannot hold it forever.

scores.title: thirty seconds of wrong
body: for thirty seconds, do something you normally wouldn't allow yourself to do.
stop. return.
scores.closing: notice if anything loosened.

scores.title: the temporary rule
body: invent a small rule for the next hour.
tell no one.
follow it completely.
let it expire.

scores.title: carry it until
body: pick up an object near you.
carry it until someone asks about it.
tell them the true reason you chose it — even if you have to invent it in the moment of telling.

scores.title: flame focus
body: light a candle for a task you want to take seriously.
blow it out when the task is done.

charms.name: stamp
charms.throughline: doesn't send anything on its own. permission to travel, but someone has to receive it.

scores.title: say it out loud
body: identify something you've been trying to figure out alone.
tell one person you haven't figured it out yet.
don't follow it with a plan.

scores.title: ask the room
body: bring a problem you've been solving alone.
ask the least likely person what they'd do.
write their answer down before you respond to it.

scores.title: borrow a certainty
body: ask someone you don't know well what they're sure of.
write it down without commentary.
carry it for the day.
scores.closing: certainty ages quickly.

scores.title: borrowed object
body: borrow something small from someone.
carry it for a day as though its history is partly yours.
return it before you explain what you were doing.

charms.name: eraser
charms.throughline: you can make marks because you have this. permission disguised as protection.

scores.title: before you're ready
body: identify something you're waiting until you're ready to do.
do a one-minute version of it now.
scores.closing: readiness is sometimes a story about the future that the body resolves in the present.

scores.title: stay in it
body: the next time you want to change the subject, don't.
stay in the thing for one more minute than feels comfortable.

scores.title: moon time
body: start a drawing.
look at it again during the next full moon.
do not finish it.

scores.title: the slow walk
body: walk somewhere you normally hurry through.
match your pace to the slowest person you can see.

charms.name: googly eye
charms.throughline: the whole site is about what you see first. the googly eye is the charm that sees back.

scores.title: peripheral vision
body: without moving your eyes, notice three things happening at the edges of your vision.
scores.closing: the center is not the only place where life occurs.

body: look out a window.
find something that was already moving before you looked.
watch it until it does something you didn't expect.
scores.closing: the world rarely waits for our attention.

scores.title: ask the object
body: pick up an object near you.
ask it silently: what do you want to be used for?
use it that way once today.
scores.closing: imagination sometimes restores function.

scores.title: the slower thought
body: write something you've been thinking about by hand, slowly.
scores.closing: notice what changes when the thought has to slow down to become legible.

charms.name: small plastic dinosaur
charms.throughline: extinct, bright green, kind of goofy. existed fully and then stopped. still somehow here.

scores.title: no record
body: do something today that will leave no trace.
don't document it. don't tell anyone.
scores.closing: let it have happened anyway.
